place: Big Coppitt Key CDP, Florida, Florida

facts.text: Big Coppitt Key’s name comes from the Old English word “coppice,” meaning a thicket of small trees that early surveyors saw on the island.
The island first linked to the mainland when Henry Flagler’s Florida East Coast Railway built a railroad fill to nearby Shark Key in the early 1900s.
Today, Big Coppitt Key is mainly a quiet residential community, home to a handful of local eateries like Bobalu’s Southern Cafe.
A key piece of infrastructure on the island is a wastewater treatment plant run by the Florida Keys Aqueduct Authority.
Geographically, the island sits in the lower Florida Keys at 24°35′52″N 81°39′24″W, just north of Key West.
Big Coppitt Key is officially a census-designated place, meaning it has no municipal government but is recognized for statistical purposes.
